purpose: Separate the beginner/AI strategy writing surface from the strict machine-readable registry, runtime adapters, and verification evidence.

folder_layout.purpose: Template-only examples for DSL, machine IR, registry specs, and oracle tests. They are not directly runnable configs.
Core Strategy Building Block registry code and deterministic export.
Future reviewed workspace-local building blocks; path-contained and never loaded as arbitrary code without validation.
Schema, registry, oracle, invariant, and WFA safety tests.

block_kinds.meaning: Computes a named numeric field such as SMA, RSI, MACD, momentum, volatility, z-score, or percentile.
Combines boolean conditions with all, any, or not.
Compares fields, constants, or computed values.
Detects a current/previous bar crossing between two operands.
Creates date/session masks or event sessions.
Refreshes target weights when signal, selection, or allocation intent changes.
Marks execution timing or accounting semantics.
Authoring scaffold that expands into a strategy_run config; not runtime calculation by itself.

layers.responsibility: Make strategy intent easy to write and review before runtime normalization.

sections.purpose: Choose an optional strategy scaffold before filling concrete config sections.
Define named computed values derived from market data, such as indicators or custom fields.
Define entry, exit, and invalidation conditions.
Define ranking and eligibility for portfolio strategies.
Define target weights, position sizing, overlays, and cash behavior.
Define when target weights are refreshed.
Define fill timing, cost, slippage, and accounting backend.

layers.responsibility: Normalize strategy intent into op-shaped nodes with no ambiguous natural language.

required_columns.name: node_id
required_columns.meaning: Stable unique id inside the strategy graph.

required_columns.name: canonical_id
required_columns.meaning: Registry canonical Strategy Building Block id.

required_columns.name: block_kind
required_columns.meaning: Registry kind used to route validation and UI explanations.

required_columns.name: usage_site
required_columns.meaning: Runtime path where this op must be supported.

required_columns.name: inputs
required_columns.meaning: Named input fields or child nodes.

required_columns.name: params_schema
required_columns.meaning: Allowed parameters from the registry.

required_columns.name: params
required_columns.meaning: Concrete parameter values or param_ref references.

required_columns.name: output_name
required_columns.meaning: Name exposed to later nodes.

required_columns.name: temporal_metadata
required_columns.meaning: Observation, availability, earliest trade, and lookback metadata.

required_columns.name: implementation_source_hash
required_columns.meaning: Hash of implementation code used for audit.

required_columns.name: evidence_paths
required_columns.meaning: Tests or docs that prove support at the usage site.

layers.responsibility: Answer whether a block is supported at a usage site and what safety metadata is required.

required_columns.name: canonical_id
required_columns.meaning: Unique op id.

required_columns.name: block_kind
required_columns.meaning: indicator, condition, calendar, rebalance trigger, execution, or template.

required_columns.name: aliases
required_columns.meaning: Accepted user/AI spellings.

required_columns.name: usage_sites
required_columns.meaning: Supported and unsupported runtime paths.

required_columns.name: params_schema
required_columns.meaning: JSON-schema-like parameter contract.

required_columns.name: temporal_metadata
required_columns.meaning: Anti-look-ahead metadata.

required_columns.name: leakage_flags
required_columns.meaning: Future-use and backfill policy.

required_columns.name: wfa_safety
required_columns.meaning: Train/OOS separation requirements.

layers.responsibility: Execute only validated IR through existing engines and adapters.

required_columns.name: engine
required_columns.meaning: MultiAssetPortfolioEngine, NodeIRExecutor, calendar resolver, or portfolio accounting path.

required_columns.name: accepted_usage_site
required_columns.meaning: Usage site already approved by registry.

required_columns.name: input_frame
required_columns.meaning: Data source and aligned frame.

required_columns.name: output_frame
required_columns.meaning: Computed indicator, mask, target weight, or trade artifact.

layers.responsibility: Prove mechanics with oracle tests, invariant tests, schema tests, and WFA safety checks.

required_columns.name: oracle_fixture
required_columns.meaning: Small deterministic data where expected trades are hand-checkable.

required_columns.name: expected_actions
required_columns.meaning: Expected entries, exits, rebalances, costs, and equity path.

required_columns.name: invariants
required_columns.meaning: Properties such as higher cost cannot improve equity.

required_columns.name: wfa_checks
required_columns.meaning: No OOS refit and train-only selection evidence.
